REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (45 Capra Way S.F.)
Off Calendar Sep-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-08-291625 Conservatorship of Oouida Davis Smith.

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Bertha Joung.

The real property is owned 50% by the Conservatorship and 50% by the Trust. The sale is for 100% of the property. The sale was confirmed at $1,685,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petitions to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the orders). An additional bond in the amount of $580,000.00 is required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K.Tauscher.

Petition For Authority To List And Sell Real Property And To Enter Into Exclusive Listing Agreement
Off Calendar May-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-08-291625 Conservatorship of Oouida Smith.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Bertha Joung.

Mr. Schultz explained that the reason for placing Ms. Smith was because the reverse mortgage was exhausted, that she has no cash to to maintain her at home and that at the end of March of this year there was $5,000.00 left in the Conservatorship and $48,000.00 left in the Trust which include the remainder of the reverse mortgage. In addition, he stated that her costs for care at home were $10,000.00 a month and that it is now about $7,000.00 a month at her placement. The Court granted both Petitions to List and Sell Real Property (subject to review of the orders).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Granted Per Written Order

FILING OF FIRST ACCOUNT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2010 Off Calendar

FILING OF INVENTORY
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Ct Supervision & Appt Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-08-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Louie for and with Herbert Smith (Mr. Smith appeared by Court Call). Lawrence Siracusa for and with Bertha Joung (Temporary Conservator). Deborah Fox for and with Martin McMahon (Trustee).

The Court stated that the Proposed Successor Trustee was Bertha Joung and that the current Trustee was Martin McMahon. Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. McMahon was prepared to submit a resignation contingent upon the appointment of Bertha Joung, addressed the issues with regard to Mr. Gillette not signing the declination, and presented additional argument to support the appointment of Bertha Joung. The Court asked if counsel knew why Mr. Gillette did not sign the declination. Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. Gillette stated he did not sign the declination because Mr. Smith wanted him to serve. Mr. Louie stated that Mr. Gillette had been given his name as the contact person by Mr. Siracusa, but he did not hear from Mr. Gillette. The Court stated that based upon all the statements made here today and with the RAM pending, it finds that Mr. Gillette refused to sign the declination and waived his right to serve as Successor Trustee by not contacting Mr. Louie. The Court granted the Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision and Appointing Bertha Joung as Successor Trustee (signing an order for the same in open court). The Court commended Mr. McMahon for his service and friendship with the Smiths as set forth on the record.

Further Orders:
1. The Inventory and Appraisal is due one week before April 22, 2009.
2. First Account & Status Report - filed one week before April 6, 2010.

Bond was set at $240,000, but was not stated on the record.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Ct Supervision & Appt Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-08-2009 Continued To Jan-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
